items.title: Spot Prices Wrap: Natural Gas Prices Rise as Winterĺs Last Gasp Targets Midwest, East
items.summary: Spot natural gas prices rose broadly Thursday for Friday delivery, drawing support from chilly air spreading across northern areas ahead of a cold front forecast to sweep across the eastern two-thirds of the country next week.

items.title: Futures Settle: Natural Gas Extends Rally as War Offsets Weak Storage Draw
items.summary: Natural gas futures eked out modest gains Thursday as the threat of prolonged disruptions to global energy supplies overshadowed a softer-than-expected U.S. storage withdrawal.

items.title: MidDay Snapshot: Spot Gas Prices Rise as Late Season Cold Boosts Demand
items.summary: Spot gas trades for Friday delivery drew support from an approaching weather system expected to return wintry conditions to key heating demand regions, while the national benchmark also firmed as the Iran war entered its 13th day.

items.title: Data Center Surge Could Spike Natural Gas Demand Beyond Forecasts, EIA Says
items.summary: The proliferation of data centers for artificial intelligence could drive U.S. natural gas consumption in the power sector significantly higher than previously estimated, according to a new analysis by the Energy Information Administration (EIA).

items.title: MidDay Futures: Gas Futures Little Changed as Storage Draw Lands Light, Cold Weather Returns

items.title: Midmorning Markets: Natural Gas Futures Shrug Off Lean EIA Storage Print
items.summary: Operators withdrew 38 Bcf of natural gas from storage for the week ended March 6, the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) reported on Thursday. The result proved lighter than market expectations and historical norms but had little immediate

items.title: Global Energy Crisis Pushes U.S. Natural Gas Forward Prices Higher Despite Mild Weather, Strong Production
items.summary: U.S. forward natural gas prices have been propped up by conflict in the Middle East as global commodity prices remain elevated and uncertainty persists over exactly how long the war will last and sustain strong demand for American LNG exports.

items.title: Gulf Coast Facing ĹGas Storage Problemĺ as LNG Exports Grow
items.summary: U.S. natural gas storage capacity is failing to keep pace with market growth overall, which can cause headaches during periods of sharp demand spikes and declines alike ľ particularly for Gulf Coast LNG exporters.

items.title: Cargo Diversions, Escalating Price War Threaten Latin American LNG Supply
items.summary: The intensifying conflict in the Middle East is upending global energy flows, with LNG vessels originally destined for Latin America now being diverted to higher-paying markets.

items.title: Canadian Crude Cashes In on War Premium

items.title: Only 3 U.S. Airlines Can Remain Profitable at Current Oil Prices

items.title: Iran Warns Gulf Energy Assets Could Burn if Its Oil Facilities Are Targeted

items.title: Tesla Wins License to Supply Electricity Across the UK
